claims.evidence: Users can export any report as CSV with a single action
Users can export any report as PDF with a single action
All exports enforce the requesting user's row-level permissions — no rows outside the user's visibility are included
Users can create a scheduled export that recurs daily or weekly
Scheduled exports are delivered via email to the schedule owner only
Schedule owners can pause and resume their schedules at any time
Failed export deliveries are retried automatically, up to 3 times
After a third failed retry, the schedule is automatically paused
Users can view the last 30 export runs (execution history) for each schedule
Export generation does not block interactive report viewing
Exports larger than 50 MB are rejected with a user-facing error message
Workspace admins can disable export functionality entirely for their workspace
Every export event (on-demand and scheduled) is recorded in the audit log
The entire feature is shipped behind a feature flag and can be toggled without redeployment

additions.summary: The feature flag can be toggled without redeployment — a runtime-toggle constraint that goes beyond C14's bare 'feature flag at launch' requirement.

claims.evidence: Export Service handles 'format rendering (CSV/PDF)'; ExportRequest domain model shows `format` (CSV | PDF); On-Demand Export workflow step 1: 'User requests export of a report in a specific format (CSV or PDF)'

Export Service handles 'format rendering (CSV/PDF)'; ExportRequest domain model shows `format` (CSV | PDF); On-Demand Export workflow step 1: 'User requests export of a report in a specific format (CSV or PDF)'

On-Demand Export step 4: 'System executes the report query with the user's row-level permission filters applied'; Execute Scheduled Export step 3: 'System executes the report query with the schedule owner's row-level permission filters'
Scheduler component 'tracks recurrence (daily/weekly)'; ScheduledExport domain model: `recurrence` (daily | weekly); Create Scheduled Export step 1: 'configures recurrence (daily or weekly)'

Delivery Workflow step 2: 'sends email with the export file attachment to the schedule owner's delivery email.' The domain model has a separate `delivery_email` field on ScheduledExport distinct from `owner_id`, and CreateScheduledExport takes `delivery_email` as an independent input — allowing delivery to an address other than the owner's own email, not 'owner only'.
PauseSchedule contract: 'PermissionDenied (only owner or admin)' — the artifact extends pause/resume rights to admins in addition to the owner, whereas the claim says 'by its owner' only.
Delivery Pipeline: 'retry logic with exponential backoff (up to 3 attempts)'; Delivery Workflow step 1: 'max 3 attempts'; step 5: 'If attempt count < 3: enqueue retry'
Delivery Workflow step 6: 'If attempt count == 3: mark execution as failed, pause the schedule, set pause_reason to "delivery_failures", log audit event for schedule pause'
Execution History Store 'retains the last 30 execution records per schedule'; View Execution History step 2: 'System retrieves the 30 most recent ExportExecution records for that schedule'
On-Demand Export step 6: 'System validates file size; if > 50 MB, reject with user-facing error'; Execute Scheduled Export step 5: 'if > 50 MB, mark execution as failed with size-exceeded reason'

Feature Flag Control component: 'gates export functionality with a workspace-level enable/disable flag that admins can toggle without redeployment'; SetExportEnabled contract: 'admin toggles export feature flag'
Audit Logger component: 'captures all export events (on-demand generation, schedule creation/modification/pause/resume, delivery success/failure, retries) with user identity, timestamp, and outcome'

additions.summary: On-demand exports can be delivered via email in addition to direct download: On-Demand Export step 7 says 'System streams the file to the user or initiates email delivery (immediate).' This is a new delivery channel for on-demand exports not covered by any claim.

A ValidateExportSize pre-flight check estimates whether an export would exceed 50 MB before full generation, so the system can fail fast without completing the render. This is an externally-visible behavior constraint beyond the reject-on-generation rule in C11.
GetExecutionHistory supports a configurable limit up to 100 records (default 30, max 100), meaning users can request more than the 30 runs mentioned in C9.

Scheduled exports always render in CSV format regardless of user preference: Execute Scheduled Export step 4 states 'System renders the result in CSV format (scheduled exports default to CSV).' PDF is not available for scheduled exports.

When export functionality is disabled by a workspace admin, all export management UI (view history, pause/resume, create schedule) becomes read-only or hidden — not just new export generation. This is a broader scope of disable than C12 describes.

The delivery_email on a ScheduledExport is a configurable field independent of the owner's email address, meaning scheduled exports can be delivered to an arbitrary email rather than only the owner's account email.

Exactly-once semantics for the scheduler component — a delivery guarantee constraint on scheduled execution.
Retention policy: execution history records are cleaned up to keep only the last 30 per schedule — a storage management behavior beyond merely displaying 30 records.
Export management UI becomes read-only (rather than fully removed) when the feature flag is disabled — a specific UI degradation behavior tied to flag state.

"If retry_count < 3: re-queue with exponential backoff" — retries happen at most 3 times (retry_count goes 0→1→2→3, triggering permanent failure at 3).
"If retry_count == 3: update status to 'failed_permanent', pause schedule, audit log records failure"
"System retrieves last 30 ExportRun records for that ScheduledExport" and Get Schedule History API with `limit` (default 30).

"Export size does not exceed 50 MB threshold" and "If size exceeds limit or generation fails: ... Return clear error message to user"; error code `ExportSizeExceeded` / `MaxExportSizeExceeded`.

"All export events are recorded in the audit log" stated in Architecture Outline, and AuditLogEntry domain model with event_type covering export_requested, export_generated, export_failed, scheduled_export_created, scheduled_export_paused, scheduled_export_resumed, export_delivered.

The artifact references a feature flag ("check feature flag", "Check Feature Enabled" API) but does not state it ships behind a feature flag at launch — feature flag is described as a runtime admin control, not a launch gate. The claim's 'at launch' condition is absent.

additions.summary: Scheduled exports allow a configurable delivery email address (not necessarily the owner's): 'Designate delivery email address (defaults to owner's email)' — any recipient email can be specified, making the owner's email merely the default.

Export download is available via a direct URL/file stream: 'Download Export' API returns a file stream (CSV or PDF), and 'Get Export Status' returns a `file_url` when ready — an on-demand pull mechanism beyond email delivery.
Failed delivery retries use exponential backoff: 'If retry_count < 3: re-queue with exponential backoff' — a specific retry timing strategy not present in the ground-truth claims.
Schedules can be deleted by their owner: 'Delete Schedule' API endpoint takes `scheduled_export_id` and `user_id` — a lifecycle operation beyond pause/resume.

Export runs track a `delivery_status` with four distinct states (pending, delivered, failed_will_retry, failed_permanent) and expose retry_count and error_message in the history view — specific observability fields for users beyond just viewing a history of runs.

When export functionality is disabled by an admin, all export requests (one-off and scheduled) are rejected with an appropriate error message — a specific behavior for the disabled state beyond just toggling the flag.
The admin audit log is queryable via a Get Audit Log API with filters (report_id, user_id, event_type, date range) — a queryable interface for audit data beyond mere recording.

Phase 3 Scope: 'Create Export Engine worker/consumer that dequeues export jobs from async job queue'; Phase 2 Scope: 'Implement POST /reports/{report_id}/exports endpoint (create one-off export, enqueue job)' — exports are async/background jobs, not blocking the report UI.
Phase 3 Scope: 'Add 50 MB size limit enforcement with graceful rejection'; Acceptance Criteria: 'Exports exceeding 50 MB are rejected with status=rejected and error="Export exceeds 50 MB limit"'.

Phase 5 Scope: 'Implement PATCH /workspace/features/scheduled_exports endpoint (toggle feature, admin-only)'; Acceptance Criteria: 'Toggling feature flag to disabled prevents new exports and schedules (API returns 403)'.

Phase 1 Scope: 'Set up audit event creation utilities'; Phase 2, 3, 4, 5 all wire audit event logging for export and schedule events. Phase 5 Acceptance Criteria: 'All schedule state changes recorded in AuditEvent table'.

Phase 1 Scope: 'Create Export, ExportSchedule, ExportRun, AuditEvent, and FeatureFlag database tables'; Phase 2 Acceptance Criteria: 'When feature flag is disabled, export endpoints return 403'; Phase 5 Scope: 'Implement GET /workspace/features/scheduled_exports endpoint (retrieve feature flag state)'.

In-app notification to schedule owner on auto-pause: Phase 4 Scope and Acceptance Criteria state 'Send in-app notification to schedule owner on auto-pause' / 'In-app notification created for schedule owner on auto-pause' — a new delivery channel/behavior not in any claim.

First delivery attempt occurs within 5 minutes of ExportRun creation: Phase 4 Acceptance Criteria states 'First delivery attempt occurs within 5 minutes of ExportRun creation' — a specific SLA constraint on delivery timing.

Schedule deletion via DELETE /schedules/{schedule_id} that also unregisters the schedule from the scheduler: Phase 5 Scope and Acceptance Criteria describe this as a distinct lifecycle operation.

additions.summary: Presigned artifact URLs are generated for export downloads, valid for 7 days (configurable). This is a new externally-visible behavior with a specific retention duration not covered by any ground-truth claim.
Retry logic uses exponential backoff with specific intervals (1 min, 5 min, 15 min for attempts 1, 2, 3), which is a delivery-timing constraint beyond the simple '3 retries' claim.
An in-app notification is sent to the schedule owner when a schedule is auto-paused after delivery failures — a new delivery channel beyond email.

A GET /audit endpoint is provided with filtering by resource_type, resource_id, event_type, and actor_id. Admins can query all events; non-admins can only query their own. This is a new user-facing audit query capability.

When the workspace feature flag is disabled, existing schedules remain in the database but are skipped by the scheduler. When re-enabled, schedules resume based on their next_scheduled_at. This is a specific behavioral constraint around feature flag toggling not in any claim.
Scheduler idempotency is guaranteed: restarting the scheduler daemon does not cause duplicate exports or deliveries.

format — CSV or PDF (domain model); 'User requests an export (CSV or PDF) of a report' (One-Off Export workflow)
'System applies the user's row-level permissions to the report query' (One-Off Export workflow step 3); 'Applies user's row-level permissions to report query' (GenerateExport API)

The artifact's delivery API is 'SendExportEmail(export_id, recipient_email, export_run_id)' with a generic recipient_email parameter; the Execute Scheduled Export workflow says 'Scheduler enqueues email delivery task' without specifying owner-only delivery. The 'owner only' constraint is absent from the delivery path.
'PauseSchedule(schedule_id, requester_id) — Returns updated ScheduledExport or 403 if not owner'; 'ResumeSchedule(schedule_id, requester_id) — Returns updated ScheduledExport or 403 if not owner'

The artifact says 'exponential retry logic (up to 3 attempts)' and 'attempt_number — 1, 2, or 3', meaning 3 total attempts (2 retries), not 3 retries (4 total attempts). The backoff example '30 seconds × 2, 4, 8 for attempts 2, 3, 4' further implies a possible 4th attempt, creating internal inconsistency, but the domain model caps attempt_number at 3.

'If attempt_number = 3 and delivery fails: Scheduler sets ScheduledExport status = paused.' The artifact pauses after the 3rd total attempt (2nd retry), whereas the claim requires pausing after the 3rd retry (4th total attempt).

additions.summary: Exports are ephemeral and may be discarded after delivery; the audit log is the permanent record (not a stored file archive).
Scheduled exports run at a user-configurable time (hour and minute) and, for weekly schedules, a specific day of the week — time-of-day and weekday targeting beyond just daily/weekly frequency.
Generation failures (as distinct from delivery failures) cause the schedule to be immediately paused without any retry — retries apply only to delivery failures.
The owner receives a failure notification email (via NotifyScheduleFailure) when a schedule is paused due to delivery failure or generation failure, including schedule details and a failure reason.
Retry delivery uses exponential backoff (base_delay × 2^attempt) rather than fixed-interval retries.
Run history records include attempt_number, timestamps (scheduled_at, started_at, completed_at), error_message, and file_size_bytes — a specific set of fields visible to users.
GetRunHistory and schedule management APIs enforce owner-only access with HTTP 403 for non-owners (scope-based access control on schedule operations).
DeleteSchedule is a supported operation that cancels pending runs and logs an audit event — schedule deletion is an explicit capability.
When workspace exports are disabled by admin, scheduled exports remain in the database but no new runs are enqueued until re-enabled (paused-in-place rather than deleted).

additions.summary: One-off export files are downloadable via a dedicated download endpoint (GET /api/export-jobs/{jobId}/download) restricted to the job owner — this is a new permission-scoped delivery mechanism not covered by any claim.
Export schedules support a user-configurable format (CSV or PDF) per schedule, selected at schedule creation time — the claim only covers format choices for one-off exports.

Export schedules include a configurable execution time of day (e.g., 09:00 UTC) and, for weekly schedules, a configurable day of week — specific time-of-day scheduling granularity is a new constraint.
Retry delivery attempts use exponential backoff between attempts — a new constraint on retry timing not present in any claim.

When a workspace admin disables exports, existing schedules remain stored in the database but are not triggered — their records are preserved rather than deleted, which is a new behavioral commitment.
The export history UI allows users to inspect individual delivery attempts (not just aggregate status) to understand failure details — a new user-facing behavior beyond viewing the 30-run history.
